title: Reversible Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: You are given a doubly linked list and asked to sort it using merge sort. The list should be sorted in ascending order. After sorting, the original order of the list elements should be preserved.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains the number of nodes in the list. Each subsequent line represents a node with its value and next node's value.
statement.outputFormat: Print the sorted list in ascending order.

hints.text: The given implementation has a bug in its merge function. The bug causes the function to lose track of the node's original position.
To solve this bug, you need to modify the merge function to correctly preserve the original order of the nodes.

editorial.title: Correcting the Merge Sort Implementation
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use the merge function to sort the sublists and then merge them in the correct order to get the final sorted list.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a temporary list to store the sorted elements.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a merge function that preserves the original order of the nodes.
commonMistakes: losing track of node's original position
